# CruxCLI — Full Content Index

> CruxCLI is a provider-agnostic terminal AI coding agent with intelligence built in. Open source (MIT), 75+ LLM providers, 24 task-specific modes, token budgets, workspace checkpoints, and CruxDev convergence engine integration. 1,204 tests passing, 13/13 packages typechecking.

---

## Homepage (https://cruxvibe.io/)

CruxCLI is a terminal AI coding agent that works with any LLM provider. 24 task-specific modes map to model tiers automatically, token budgets replace step-count limits, and workspace checkpoints protect your code. Open source, self-hostable, 1,204 tests passing.

### Trust metrics
- 1,204 tests passing
- 13/13 packages typecheck
- 75+ LLM providers
- 24 task-specific modes

### Problem / Solution
Without CruxCLI: One model for everything. Step-count limits. No safety net. Locked to one provider. No convergence signal.
With CruxCLI: Mode-to-model tier mapping. Token budgets. Workspace checkpoints. 75+ providers. CruxDev convergence.

### Six unique advantages
1. Mode → Model Tier Mapping: 24 modes automatically select the right model tier. Debugging gets a stronger model than formatting.
2. Token Budget System: Per-mode token budgets replace step-count limits. Warning at 75%, hard stop at 90%.
3. CruxDev Convergence Engine: Autonomous audit-fix-re-audit loops until two consecutive clean passes.
4. Workspace Checkpoints: Automatic git-based snapshots before every destructive tool call. Up to 50 per project.
5. Client/Server + LSP: HTTP server on port 4096 with 30+ language server integrations.
6. Crux Intelligence Layer: 24 mode prompts, correction detection, continuous learning — native in binary.

### How it works
1. Install: npm i -g cruxcli
2. Configure: export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-...
3. Launch: cruxcli
4. Build: > refactor auth module

### Supported providers
OpenRouter, Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Ollama, Azure, Bedrock, Vertex, xAI, Mistral, Groq, DeepInfra, Cerebras, Cohere, TogetherAI, Perplexity, and 60+ more.

---

## Features (https://cruxvibe.io/features)

CruxCLI is a provider-agnostic terminal AI coding agent with 24 task-specific modes, per-mode token budgets, workspace checkpoints, client/server architecture, LSP integration for 30+ languages, and MCP plugin support.

### Mode → Model Tier Mapping
24 task-specific modes each map to a model tier. Code review uses a strong reasoning model. Formatting uses a fast cheap model. No other terminal agent does this.

### Token Budget System
Per-mode token budgets replace step-count limits. Every mode has a configurable token budget with warning at 75% and hard limit at 90%. Step counts are a blunt instrument. Token budgets measure actual cost.

### Workspace Checkpoints
Before every destructive tool call (edit, write, bash, patch), CruxCLI takes a git-based snapshot. Up to 50 checkpoints per project. Restore with cruxcli checkpoint restore.

### Client/Server Architecture
Local HTTP server (Hono-based, port 4096). TUI, web app, desktop app (Tauri/Electron), and VS Code extension connect as clients. Only CruxCLI and OpenCode have this architecture.

### LSP Integration
Built-in Language Server Protocol client supporting 30+ language servers. Real diagnostics, symbol lookup, go-to-definition. Only CruxCLI and OpenCode have LSP integration.

### Plugins and MCP
Full MCP client (stdio, SSE, StreamableHTTP). Plugin API for custom tools. Built-in plugins for Codex auth, Copilot auth, GitLab auth.

### Provider-Agnostic
75+ LLM providers. Switch by changing one environment variable. Runtime model fetching from models.dev with offline fallback.

### CruxDev Integration
Convergence engine via MCP. Autonomous audit-fix-re-audit loops. Two consecutive clean passes = converged.

---

## CruxCLI vs OpenCode (https://cruxvibe.io/vs/opencode)

CruxCLI is a hard fork of OpenCode that adds an intelligence layer. Both are open source, provider-agnostic, with 75+ provider support, LSP integration, and plugin APIs. CruxCLI adds 24 modes with model tier mapping, token budgets, checkpoints, and convergence. OpenCode has 129,357 stars and 800+ contributors.

Where CruxCLI wins: Intelligence layer (24 modes, model tier mapping), token budgets, workspace checkpoints, CruxDev convergence.
Where OpenCode wins: Community (129k stars, 800+ contributors), GitLab integration, free Zen tier, near-daily releases.

---

## CruxCLI vs Claude Code (https://cruxvibe.io/vs/claude-code)

CruxCLI is open source (MIT), provider-agnostic (75+ providers), with 24 modes and token budgets. Claude Code is proprietary, Anthropic-only, with first-party Opus 4.6 access, 1M context window, and 64k/128k output limits.

Where CruxCLI wins: Open source, provider-agnostic, plugin API, LSP, mode system, convergence.
Where Claude Code wins: First-party Opus 4.6 access, 1M context window, cost visibility, multi-agent workflows, brand recognition (82k stars).

---

## CruxCLI vs Gemini CLI (https://cruxvibe.io/vs/gemini-cli)

CruxCLI is provider-agnostic with 24 modes, token budgets, and convergence. Gemini CLI is Google-only with a free tier, 1M context, and Google Search grounding. 98,907 stars in under two months.

Where CruxCLI wins: 24 modes, convergence, provider flexibility, LSP, plugins.
Where Gemini CLI wins: Free tier, Google Search grounding, community (99k stars), 1M context window.

---

## CruxCLI vs Codex CLI (https://cruxvibe.io/vs/codex-cli)

CruxCLI has LSP integration, plugin API, client/server architecture, and 24 modes. Codex CLI is Rust-based with multi-agent workflows and image input, locked to OpenAI.

Where CruxCLI wins: LSP (30+ servers), plugin API, client/server, provider flexibility.
Where Codex CLI wins: Rust performance, multi-agent workflows, screenshot input, OpenAI backing (67k stars).

---

## Roadmap (https://cruxvibe.io/roadmap)

### Completed
- Hard fork from OpenCode (BUILD_PLAN_001)
- Workspace checkpoints (BUILD_PLAN_003)
- VS Code extension (BUILD_PLAN_004)
- Competitive analysis (COMPETITORS.md)
- Key migration documentation (BUILD_PLAN_005)

### In Progress
- API key migration (environment variables → OS keychain)
- CruxDev integration hardening
- Plugin ecosystem formalization

### Planned
- Browser automation mode
- Enterprise compliance tier
- Memory pipeline
- Cost visibility
- Image/screenshot input

### Non-Goals
- Upstream sync with OpenCode (clean break)
- Building what can be integrated (use MCP servers)
- Node.js runtime support (Bun-only)
- Free hosted service (local tool, BYOK)

---

## Token Budgets (https://cruxvibe.io/lp/no-execution-cap)

Most AI coding agents stop mid-task because of step-count execution limits (25-50 tool calls). CruxCLI replaces step-count limits with per-mode token budgets that measure actual token consumption. Warning at 75%, graceful wrap-up, hard stop at 90%. Each of 24 modes has its own budget matched to task complexity.

---

## Provider-Agnostic (https://cruxvibe.io/lp/provider-agnostic)

CruxCLI supports 75+ LLM providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Azure, Bedrock, Vertex, OpenRouter, Ollama, and more. Switch by changing one environment variable. Mode → model tier mapping works across all providers. Local models via Ollama for air-gapped environments.

---

## Convergence (https://cruxvibe.io/lp/convergence)

CruxCLI integrates with CruxDev's convergence engine. Autonomous audit-fix-re-audit loops run until two consecutive independent clean passes verify code correctness. No other terminal AI agent has convergence methodology. All others rely on model judgment or user decision.

---

## Technical Details

- Binary size: ~110MB compiled
- Runtime: Bun
- Architecture: Client/server (Hono HTTP server on port 4096)
- Database: SQLite (Drizzle ORM)
- TUI: Ink (React for CLI)
- Frontends: TUI, Web, Desktop (Tauri/Electron), VS Code
- LSP: 30+ language servers auto-detected
- MCP: stdio, SSE, StreamableHTTP transports
- Checkpoints: Git tree hashing, up to 50 per project
- Tests: 1,204 passing (Vitest)
- Typecheck: 13/13 packages (TypeScript)
- License: MIT
- Organization: Trinsik Labs
- Website: https://cruxvibe.io
- GitHub: https://github.com/trinsiklabs/cruxcli
